name: Thai Pad Thai (Stir Fried Noodles)
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces rice vermicelli (sen mee or sen lek style of Thai noodles)
30 minutes - 1 hour soaking time until soft
Quarter cup dried shrimp, rolled or roughly pounded
Half cup roasted peanuts, coarsely ground
Half a cup fried tofu marinated in dark sweet soy
or equivalent amount of coarsely chopped pork or chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat cooking oil in a wok and add garlic and shallots. Briefly stir fry until they change color.
2. Add remaining ingredients except egg and bean sprouts, and stir fry until protein ingredient is nearly cooked.
3. Slowly drizzle beaten egg to form a fine ribbon of cooked egg. If not confident, make an egg crepe, roll it up, slice into quarters, and add to the mix.
4. Add bean sprouts and cook for no more than 30 seconds.
5. Remove from pan to serving platter.
6. Mix lime juice, tamarind juice, and fish sauce in a bowl. Marinate uncooked bean sprouts, chives, and peanuts with this mixture. Sprinkle over cooked pad thai.
7. Cut limes into segments and cucumber rounds. Arrange around the serving platter.
8. Optional: Decorate with sliced banana flower and Indian Pennywort leaves.
9. Serve with copious amounts of four basic condiments (chilies in fish sauce, ground red chili, sugar, crushed peanuts) at the table.
Khao Koor:
1. Get a medium-sized wok hot and add uncooked rice. Keep moving until it turns golden brown.
2. Remove from heat and allow to cool.
3. Grind to a coarse powder using a spice mill or mortar and pestle.
4. Use 1 cup of dry roasted, unsalted peanuts (roasted in shells on charcoal brazier, oven, or skillet).
